title: Kaiming He
excerpts: **Kaiming He** ( Chinese : 何恺明 ; pinyin : *Hé Kǎimíng* ) is a Chinese computer scientist who primarily researches computer vision and deep learning . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> He is an associate professor at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and works part-time as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> He is known as one of the creators of the residual neural network (ResNet) architecture. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

title: Xingjian Bai | 白行健
excerpts:
Section Title: **Xingjian Bai | 白行健**
Content:
Hello! I am a first-year Ph.D. student at MIT, supervised by [Prof. Kaiming He](https://people.csail.mit.edu/kaiming/) . I received my master's and bachelor's degree in Mathematics and Computer Science from the University of Oxford.
My research has focused on the intersection of classic algorithms and deep learning, covering topics such as physics-inspired generative models and learning-augmented algorithms.
More broadly, I am driven by research that is scientifically impactful and intellectually stimulating.

title: Xianbang Wang
excerpts:
Currently, I am an undergraduate researcher supervised by Professor Kaiming He, working on improving the performance and generalizability of modern deep generative models.
I am a first-year undergraduate at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Class of 2029. I intend to double major in Mathematics & Computer Science and AI & Decision Making, with a minor in Physics.
Selected Publications > Bidirectional Normalizing Flow: From Data to Noise and Back — Authors: ... X. Wang ... Status: Submitted to CVPR 2026

title: Biography:Kaiming He - HandWiki
excerpts:
Section Title: Biography : Kaiming He > Namespaces > Page actions
Content:
**Kaiming He** ( Chinese : 何恺明 ; pinyin : *Hé Kǎimíng* ) is a Chinese computer scientist who primarily researches computer vision and deep learning . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> He is an associate professor at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and works part-time as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> He is known as one of the creators of residual neural network (ResNet). <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

title: Kaiming He（Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, Associate Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and recipient of the Future Science Prize.）_Baiduwiki
excerpts:
English
Key Information
Kaiming He
Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, Associate Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and recipient of the Future Science Prize.
The most cited AI paper of the 21st century was written by this Chinese genius(from:量子位)
12:11
Kaiming He, born in 1984 in Guangzhou, Guangdong, is an artificial intelligence scientist, a Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, an associate professor (with tenure) and the Douglas Ross Software Technology Career Development Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and concurrently serves as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind.
1
